Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Experimental Procedures state the shRNA primers were designed based on RBOHD gene sequences Sotub06g025550 and Sotub06g025580.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Figure 1c and text describe RBOHD as a respiratory burst oxidase homolog; Discussion identifies RBOH proteins as NADPH oxidases.

Source_Species.reason: The study is performed in potato and Figure 1c explicitly names potato respiratory burst oxidase homolog D.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Keywords and Plant material identify the species as Solanum tuberosum.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Plant material and Results state the cultivar used is cv. Rywal, including for transgenic derivation.

Applied_Species.reason: Functional testing of RBOHD silencing was done in potato plants.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied experimental system is Solanum tuberosum.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: shRBOHD lines were generated in cv. Rywal background.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The paper directly measures salicylic acid and H2O2/ROS in relation to RBOHD and concludes RBOHD affects SA spatial accumulation; ROS are central to RBOHD function.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: SA is a phytohormone and H2O2/ROS are reactive oxygen species, both explicitly discussed.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Results and Summary link SA and ROS signaling with defense signaling and spatial restriction of PVY spread.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: RBOHD expression peaks at the lesion border, SA was measured in lesion section A and surrounding section B, and H2O2 accumulation was assessed around lesions.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show shRBOHD plants fail to arrest virus spread, have increased lesion number and viral abundance, systemic spread, and altered spatial SA accumulation.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All listed points are stated in Results: border-specific induction, reduction in NahG, compromised resistance upon silencing, unchanged lesion-center SA but altered surrounding SA, and UGT76D1 deregulation from RNA-seq.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are explicitly used in Results/Methods for RBOHD: spatial qPCR, shRNA silencing, viral qPCR, lesion counts, GC-MS SA measurements, RNA-seq, and DAB staining.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Figure 4 and text state RBOHD responds in section A early and later peaks close to the lesion border during lesion development.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Results state RBOHD forms a peak near the lesion border and is close to the limit of quantification in NahG plants.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: The paper discusses RBOH/NADPH oxidases as generating apoplastic ROS and cites plasma membrane activation in general, but it does not explicitly state potato RBOHD subcellular localization in this study.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: The paper identifies functional relationship between RBOHD, SA, and UGT76D1, but does not demonstrate protein-protein interactions. UGT76D1 is a functionally linked gene, not an interacting protein shown here.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: RBOHD was studied under PVYN-Wilga, PVYNTN, and PVYN-GFP infection and under SA depletion in NahG plants.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Altered SA spatial regulation and broader H2O2 response are supported, but framing these as tradeoffs is interpretive and not explicitly presented as a breeding/fitness tradeoff in Results.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper indeed treats RBOHD as an immune signaling component; shRNA design targeted a consensus of two RBOHD loci, explicitly stated as Sotub06g025550 and Sotub06g025580.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The Discussion implies importance for sustainable resistance and identifies RBOHD as key, but no explicit breeding or engineering application claim for RBOHD is tested or stated in Results.

Omics_Data.reason: RNA-seq data are reported and GEO accession GSE142002 is explicitly provided.

corrections.old_value: UGT76D1 (functional feedback-associated gene); SA signaling module
corrections.reason: The paper identifies functional relationship between RBOHD, SA, and UGT76D1, but does not demonstrate protein-protein interactions. UGT76D1 is a functionally linked gene, not an interacting protein shown here.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Results explicitly identify UDP-glucosyltransferase UGT76D1 as Sotub10g024000.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The gene is explicitly described as a UDP-glucosyltransferase.

Source_Species.reason: UGT76D1 was identified in potato tissue by RNA-seq in this study.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The study organism is Solanum tuberosum.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: RNA-seq included cv. Rywal and derived transgenics.

Applied_Species.reason: The observations for UGT76D1 come from potato plants.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied species is Solanum tuberosum.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The relevant NT background is cv. Rywal, with shRBOHD and NahG derived from it.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: UGT76D1 is discussed specifically in relation to salicylic acid accumulation and feedback activation of SA biosynthesis.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Salicylic acid is the phytohormone measured and discussed.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: SA is presented as part of defense signaling in resistance to PVY.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: SA was measured in lesion section A and surrounding section B; UGT76D1 expression was also compared in sections A and B.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results state UGT76D1 is strongly spatially deregulated in shRBOHD plants and could underlie altered SA accumulation pattern.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points are directly reflected in Results and Figure 7b/c, though the mechanistic role is proposed rather than functionally proven.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Spatial RNA-seq was used to detect UGT76D1 changes, compared across NT, shRBOHD, and NahG, alongside SA measurements in sections.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results state silencing of RBOHD results in induction of UGT76D1 in section B.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Figure 7b and text support higher UGT76D1 expression in adjacent tissue of shRBOHD compared with NT.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: The paper shows indirect functional linkage to RBOHD/SA but does not report protein interaction data for UGT76D1 with RBOHD or any proteins.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: UGT76D1 was examined after PVYN-Wilga infection in NT, NahG, and shRBOHD plants.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly presents UGT76D1 as a novel feedback-associated component of SA spatial regulation and does not perform direct transgenic functional validation of UGT76D1 itself.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Potential utility is speculative; the paper does not test or explicitly propose engineering UGT76D1 for resistance improvement.

Omics_Data.reason: UGT76D1 evidence comes from RNA-seq, and GEO accession GSE142002 is explicitly given.

corrections.reason: The paper shows indirect functional linkage to RBOHD/SA but does not report protein interaction data for UGT76D1 with RBOHD or any proteins.
